_comment: RAT state/v1 conformance vectors — ADR-003 shared golden data. The SINGLE source of truth for the state-backend axis (a tier-0 plugin). Both independent references (Go: plugins/state/inmemory-go, Python: plugins/state/inmemory-py) load THIS file and drive StateService (Get/Put/List unary + Watch server-streaming, the latter via the ADR-008 InvokeServerStream relay) over real gRPC. The STATEFUL lifecycle exercises: compare-and-set via the PutOutcome enum (COMMITTED on match/unconditional, CONFLICT on revision mismatch — a CAS conflict is a NORMAL outcome, not a gRPC error), Get found/not-found + revision, List under a prefix, and an ordered Watch replay of the change log. The errors array exercises the KEY GRAMMAR (freeze-blocker #3 / SEC-2): empty / oversize(>512B via key_len) / NUL / control-char / path-traversal keys -> INVALID_ARGUMENT. NOTE on encoding: control-char keys are specified via key_len (build 'a'*N) and key_inject (build 'a' + chr(N) + 'b') so this file stays pure-ASCII valid JSON — no raw control bytes. Round-1 scope: in-memory (CAS is trivially linearizable, never UNKNOWN); the semantic CAS-under-contention test is round-2 (state=sqlite). The revision sequence is deterministic so both impls stay in lockstep: each COMMITTED Put bumps a global counter (first commit -> revision 1); a CONFLICT does NOT bump.
